$comment: Canonical cross-device QA matrix (P10 — uplevel/10-device-quality-net.md). Synced per app by `sync.mjs qa`. The matrix is capped at <=6 devices FOREVER — coverage breadth beats axis explosion. 'store' maps each device onto render-screenshots.mjs surfaces + capture.mjs store keys. 'sim'/'avd' are best-effort identifiers: capture.mjs greps `simctl` for the sim (creating it if absent) and, for Android, targets the named avd or the first booted emulator. If your installed sims/avds differ, drop a gitignored qa/devices.local.json with the same shape to override — never edit this synced file per-app.

axes.$comment: appearance + fontScale are applied at the device level before each traverse (simctl ui / adb settings); orientation is injected into the generated flow as a Maestro setOrientation (compile-flow --orientation), so it drives the OS deterministically on both platforms. Orientation is per-kind: phones portrait-only, tablets portrait + landscape.

smoke.$comment: Per-PR gate. Identical cost to today's CI: one phone per platform, light appearance, default font, portrait. Adding nothing here keeps the PR gate cheap.

full.$comment: Per-release gate + monthly fleet-health re-run. Every device; appearance x fontScale crossed; landscape added on tablets by the orientation axis. One hash-cached build per platform is reused across every cell — only the (cheap) device reconfigure + traverse repeats.
